titleStmt.title: SII 23.506: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 506. (A.R. No. 506 of 1907.) Madurai, Madurai Taluk, Madurai District Madanagōpāla temple—on eight other stones in the same place

summary.p: These are disconnected pieces of a few inscriptions. One of these is dated in the 11+1st year of a king whose surname Māṟavarman alone is found. King Parākrama-Pāṇḍyadēva is mentioned in a piece which might be a part of this record, and which registers a royal order granting some land as dēvadāna to a temple. Another piece mentions a certain••• Pāṇḍya-Nṛittappēraiyaṉ. A still different piece seems to be a record of a gift of land made by (?) one Śivagōchari-Mahādēvaṉ of the Bhāradvāja-gōtram known by the appellation Śaivāchārya.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . komāṟapaṉmarāṉa tiripuvaṉacca—vaṟku yāṇṭu patiṉoṉ ṟāvatiṉ Etirāmā—
pūrvapakṣattu peṟṟa Attattuṉāḷ—racaṅ kaāñaiyirukkai me nallūr nāyaṉār yuṭaiya pa
śrīrudraśrīma ttaṉa mutalikaḷil— ppiṭipāṭupaṇṇikkuṭutta paricāvatu
kkalum Aruḷāḷaṉ ke ṭṭār pakkaluñ ciritaraṉeriyi— ṉeri yum parākkiramapāṇṭiya tevar pā
ṉ kelkaikkuṭpaṭṭatum ṉārkkut tevatāṉam Ākak kalve— ḷaṅkaḷukkut tiruvāykkeḻviyun tirumukamun

—pāṇṭiya niruttap peraiyaṉcey
—lil tanakku ndhanmamāy varukiṟa naṭa
—ṟu paḻaiyapaṭiye peṟaveṇume
—kaṭaviya cammutimaiyum muṉpu śrīva
—ṇṭu paṇi celutti Ivaṉ viṭṭup
—ṟeveṇumeṉṟum Ikkoyil va

—tukkuk kaṭamai Antarāyam viṉiyokaṅ kaṟpūravilai poṉvari veṭṭipāṭṭam pa—li cantivikkirakappeṟu tiruvi—laicciṉaippeṟu tiruvācaṟ
—morumāvukkut tiramam yiraṇṭiṉālaccu Araikkālum vaḷaiyiṟ kālāl nellaiṅkalamuṅ �—nākavum puṉcey payireṟiṉa ni—lam payirpārttup payir ni
—pu yippaṟṟukkuḷḷuk kuṭiyiruntavarkaḷukku maṉaiyiṟai talaiyoppukak koḷḷāppo—ppaṭi cammatittup piṭipāṭupa—ṇṇikkuṭuttom tevakā
-ṟku Ināyaṉārkku śevācārya bhāradvāji gotrattu śivagocari mahādevaṉ Uḷ— śaivātarājaṉ Eḻuttu I-nayiṉār devakaṉmipaṭṭaṉ
Digital edition of SII 23.506 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
